svg.title: Sender Reputation Model
svg.desc: Sender reputation model: mailbox providers continuously evaluate a sender's cumulative trust score by weighing positive signals against negative signals. Positive signals include opens and reads, replies and forwards, spam folder rescues (not-spam corrections), consistent sending patterns, and clean list hygiene with low bounce rates. Negative signals include spam reports, hard bounces, spam trap hits, blocklist appearances, and consistently low engagement rates. These signals converge into a sender reputation score that determines whether messages are placed in the inbox, promotions tab, or spam folder. After placement, recipient behavior generates new engagement data — positive actions like opens and replies feed back as positive signals, while spam complaints feed back as negative signals, creating a continuous feedback cycle.

cc:Work.dc:title: Sender Reputation Model Diagram
cc:Work.dc:creator: InboxAlly
cc:Work.dc:subject: sender reputation, email engagement, opens, reads, replies, forwards, spam rescues, not spam, sending patterns, list hygiene, bounce rate, spam complaints, spam traps, blocklists, low engagement, inbox placement, promotions tab, spam folder, email deliverability, reputation feedback cycle
cc:Work.dc:description: Balance model showing how positive and negative signals converge into a cumulative sender reputation score, determine placement outcomes, and cycle back through post-delivery engagement.

g.aria-label: Positive signal: opens and reads indicate active inbox engagement
g.data-description: Recipients opening and reading emails signals relevance to mailbox providers

g.aria-label: Positive signal: replies and forwards are the highest-trust engagement actions
g.data-description: Replying to or forwarding a message is one of the strongest positive signals

g.aria-label: Positive signal: spam folder rescues explicitly correct filtering and restore trust
g.data-description: Recipients moving messages from spam to inbox is a direct positive correction signal

g.aria-label: Positive signal: consistent sending volume and patterns demonstrate reliable sender behavior
g.data-description: Predictable volume, proper warming, and steady cadence signal reliable sender behavior

g.aria-label: Positive signal: clean list hygiene with low bounce rates indicates quality sending practices
g.data-description: Low bounce rates and active valid recipients demonstrate responsible list management

g.aria-label: Negative signal: spam reports are the strongest negative signal and directly damage reputation
g.data-description: Recipients marking messages as spam is the most damaging signal

g.aria-label: Negative signal: hard bounces to invalid addresses indicate poor list management
g.data-description: Sending to invalid addresses signals poor list quality

g.aria-label: Negative signal: spam trap hits indicate list sourcing or hygiene problems
g.data-description: Sending to trap addresses indicates serious list quality or sourcing issues

g.aria-label: Negative signal: blocklist appearances severely damage deliverability and trust
g.data-description: IP or domain appearing on blocklists severely restricts deliverability

g.aria-label: Negative signal: persistently low engagement rates signal unwanted or irrelevant mail
g.data-description: Persistent low open and interaction rates signal content is unwanted

g.aria-label: Sender reputation: cumulative trust score calculated by weighing positive signals against negative signals
g.data-description: Cumulative trust score combining positive and negative signals over time
Strong reputation results in inbox placement

g.aria-label: Outcome: Message delivered to promotions tab
g.data-description: Marketing content categorized into promotions tab

g.aria-label: Outcome: Message delivered to spam folder
g.data-description: Weak reputation results in spam folder placement

g.aria-label: Post-placement: recipient engagement generates new positive and negative signals that feed back into reputation
g.data-description: Recipient behavior after delivery generates new signals that feed back into reputation

g.aria-label: Feedback: positive engagement actions feed back as positive signals for future messages
Feedback: spam complaints feed back as negative signals for future messages
